generated: Joel Farabee is still finding his way into Calgary’s lineup, playing limited minutes when he does get called up. His role remains largely supportive as he adjusts to life with a new team.

Jesperi Kotkaniemi is a young, reliable two-way center who helps his team control play in all situations. He’s strong at both ends of the ice and continues to grow as one of Carolina’s most trusted players.
Alexander “Sasha” Holtz is an exciting young winger who brings speed and skill to Vegas’s lineup. He plays with energy, making smart reads and contributing offense when given opportunities.
Jamie “Oiler” Olekiak is a veteran defenseman known for his size and shot-blocking ability. He plays a simple, physical game that makes him valuable in defensive zone situations.

Phillip Danault continues to be an elite defensive forward, leading all forwards in blocked shots and posting strong assist numbers as well. His high giveaway rate reflects his active puck movement but also shows some risky plays at times.
Tyson Foerster is developing into an effective net-front presence, generating rebounds and second-chance opportunities with his willingness to play in tight spaces.
Joel Edmundson is a stay-at-home defenseman who plays a simple, physical game. His value comes from his defensive positioning and ability to limit quality chances against.
Kurtis MacDermid plays a tough, physical game that helps set the tone for New Jersey’s defensive zone. He provides depth and energy in a limited role but adds valuable grit when called upon.

Adam Henrique is an elite defensive center who blocks more shots than any forward in the league. He plays a responsible two-way game that helps his team control play whenever he's on the ice, making him one of Edmonton's most valuable forwards.

Colton Parayko is an elite offensive defenseman who thrives in transition. He joins the rush with speed, creates rebounds from the point, and scores timely goals to drive St. Louis’s offense from the blue line.

Juhas Kaikkonen has been one of Carolina’s most effective forwards, dominating puck possession and driving play in all situations. His elite numbers reflect his ability to control games from start to finish.
Erik Carlsson is a big, physical defenseman who plays with an edge. He isn’t afraid to throw his body around and can be effective at drawing penalties from opponents when he uses his size correctly.
Tage Thompson is an elite goal scorer who thrives in front of the net. He scores from rebounds and high-volume shots, blocks shots defensively, and drives play forward for Buffalo’s offense.
At age 36, Alex Ovechkin continues to defy expectations with elite goal-scoring numbers and heavy shot volume. His combination of power and patience remains unmatched in today's NHL.
Vladislav Gavrikov is a reliable, stay-at-home defenseman who thrives in defensive situations and protects his team's end of the ice effectively when he's out there.

Alex Leaf is an energetic, high-event forward who plays with great intensity. He drives play towards his own net and creates second-chance opportunities through aggressive forechecking and hard work in front of the opposition’s crease.

Tanner Pearson brings veteran leadership and reliable two-way play to Vegas’s lineup. His experience and versatility make him a valuable depth forward who can fill in seamlessly across multiple lines.
Morgan Frost is an agile two-way forward who excels at stripping opponents of the puck. His quick hands and positioning make him a reliable defensive presence for Calgary’s attack.

Jack Roslovic is an elite play driver who tilts the ice in his team’s favor every shift he takes. His ability to control puck possession and drive quality chances makes him one of Carolina’s most valuable forwards.

Mikkel Boedker has been an effective veteran winger for Dallas, providing reliable two-way play and consistent offense from his third-line role. His experience and versatility have helped balance out the Stars’ depth chart.

Matthew “Tank” Tkuchuk is one of the most dominant and entertaining players in hockey today. He’s an elite possession driver who controls play at both ends, scoring goals from high-danger areas while also ranking among league leaders in penalty minutes.
Mason McMichael is an aggressive young forward who plays with edge and emotion. His high penalty total reflects his willingness to engage physically, though he can occasionally overstep boundaries.

Nikita Zadorov is one of the league’s most physical defenders, ranking among the top players in both hits and penalty draws. His punishing style sets the tone for Boston’s defense and wears down opponents over the course of a game.

William “Wild William Carrier is one of hockey’s ultimate energy players. He brings physicality, forechecking pressure, and relentless effort every shift, helping his team control possession and wear down opponents night after night.
Jack McBain plays with an edge, ranking among leaders in hits and penalty minutes. His physical style complements his hard-working approach to every shift.

Nick Suzuki is an elite playmaker and two-way center who drives offensive production at a remarkable rate. He consistently ranks among the league’s best in assists, points, and expected goals created, showing exceptional vision and leadership for Montreal.
Luke Scott Schen is one of the league's most prolific hitters from the blue line, delivering more than three hundred checks per season. His physical game sets him apart as an enforcer on defense.

Zach Werenski is an elite offensive defenseman who drives play from the blue line. He fires pucks from everywhere, generates rebounds, and chips in consistently from the point, making him one of the most prolific scoring defenders in the league.

Scott Laughton is an energy-driven middle six forward who brings grit and physicality to Toronto’s lineup. His game revolves around hard work, forechecking pressure, and willingness to throw his body around in all three zones.

Nathan MacKinnon is one of the most dominant offensive players in the league, controlling play at an elite level while racking up massive point totals. He fires pucks from everywhere and creates rebounds with incredible frequency, driving possession numbers off the charts for Colorado.
Jamie Oleksiak continues to play physical hockey, ranking near the top of the league in penalty minutes. His tough style remains central to his game and identity.
Evgenii Dadonov is an experienced veteran who brings depth and versatility to Dallas’s forward group. His ability to play multiple positions allows the team flexibility in its lineup construction.

Brad Marchand continues to be one of hockey’s most polarizing players, mixing high-end offensive creativity with an aggressive style that often leads to penalties. His ability to draw and take calls remains unmatched among modern forwards, though his prolific giveaway rate shows how risky he can be with the puck in transition.
Connor Clifton is a hard-nosed defenseman who plays with an edge. He leads by example through physical play and a willingness to block shots, making him a popular choice among teammates.

Zach Hyman is one of the most effective net-front players in hockey, thriving off rebounds and high-danger chances. His game revolves around positioning, work rate, and finishing around the crease, making him an elite goal scorer from difficult areas.
Alex Vlasic is a young, mobile defenseman who excels at puck retrieval and creating turnovers. He blocks shots and helps his team control play in transition with smart positioning.

Mathew Barzal is one of the league’s most dominant possession players, with elite control over both Fenwick and Corsi metrics. His team consistently dominates shot quality whenever he is on the ice, highlighting his ability to drive play at an exceptional level.

Jason Robertson continues to dominate offensively, ranking among elite players in goals and rebound conversions. His high giveaway rate shows an aggressive style that can create turnovers but also drives scoring chances.
Michael McCarron is a tough, physical forward who plays with an edge. He brings energy and grit to every shift but often finds himself in penalty trouble due to his aggressive style of play.

Tyson Jost is an efficient, responsible depth forward who helps his team control play and possesses the puck effectively in all situations. His ability to win battles and read developing plays makes him a reliable presence for Carolina down the middle.
Devon Toews offensive instincts shine through his strong assist and point totals, though he remains active defensively with high takeaway numbers.

Jesper Bratt elite playmaking and two-way instincts continue to shine. Hes one of New Jerseys most reliable drivers of offense, consistently setting up teammates while also contributing heavily himself with strong finishing ability

Erik Gudbranson is an offensive-minded defenseman who thrives in transition. He moves the puck quickly and supports the rush, but can be exposed defensively when pressed into heavy minutes against top competition.
Pulock’s shot-blocking numbers remain strong, reflecting his willingness to step up in traffic and take away chances for opponents.

Jaden Schwartz combination of playmaking and finishing touch continues to shine in Seattle. Hes quietly become one of their most reliable scorers, combining quick hands with smart positioning to create chances from all over the ice.
